overview: Glitter Bogles is a Pauper combo deck that focuses on attaching multiple powerful auras to a hexproof creature to create an unblockable, lethal threat. While it contains aggro elements, its win condition relies on setting up specific key pieces to reach inevitability rather than winning purely through combat. The deck utilizes mana fixing and card draw to consistently assemble its combo while navigating a meta heavy on removal and enchantment hate.
win_conditions: Deal lethal combat damage with a massive hexproof creature buffed by multiple auras
Survive aggressive decks by utilizing lifelink auras to gain life and outvalue opponents
Race combo decks by assembling the aura combo faster than the opponent can win

keep_criteria: Hands containing creatures to develop the game plan
Adequate land drops to ensure mana consistency
Cards that guarantee mana fixing for the splash colors
Keep any hand containing a land plus Bogles.
Keep land + Bogles + Utopia Sprawl/Wild Growth or Gilt-Leaf/Elvish Archdruid.
mulligan_criteria: Hands that cannot dig through the deck for key pieces
Risky hands without auras that leave the board vulnerable to wipes
Mulligan anything without a Bogles or without a land.

examples.reason: Article states this hand has potential but will likely put you in an irreversible situation before finding a creature, making the risk not worth it.
Article notes this hand is playable but risky due to lacking an aura to buff creatures, exposing the board to wipes, and inability to dig for missing pieces.

early_game: Prioritize playing lands and fixing mana to access splash colors
Deploy creatures early to establish a board presence
Play around early removal and edicts by holding up mana or sequencing carefully
Turn 2: Play land, cast Bogles, cast Utopia Sprawl (or Gilt-Leaf/Elvish Archdruid).
If you have a druid, cast it, then cast Utopia Sprawl/Wild Growth on Bogles.
Turn 3: Cast Wild Growth/Utopia Sprawl on Bogles, attack for lethal.
mid_game: Attach multiple auras like Ethereal Armor and All That Glitters to hexproof creatures
Use ramp and draw spells like Utopia Sprawl and Commune with Spirits to find missing combo pieces
Navigate board wipes and enchantment hate by mulliganing appropriately or playing around known threats
always prioritize untapping lands to fuel the next turn's attack.
Keep cards in hand for protection or finishing moves.
Use Malevolent Rumble strategically to dig for Utopia Sprawl/Wild Growth or answers.
Commit your Bogles early and often.
Never hold back a massive attack unless you have a clear answer to a specific threat (like a board wipe or targeted removal).
late_game: Push for lethal damage with the buffed hexproof creature
Utilize lifelink to stabilize against aggressive decks
Race combo decks by maximizing damage output in available windows
A single Bogles with Utopia Sprawl(s) and Wild Growth(s) for 10+ damage.
Malevolent Rumble can close the game if you have enough mana and a creature to attack with.
Go all-in when you have enough mana to cast your next Sprawl/Growth and attack for lethal, or when the opponent has no interaction left.
Never hold back a lethal attack.

key_cards.name: Ethereal Armor
key_cards.notes: Essential for increasing power/toughness to reach lethal quickly.

key_cards.name: All That Glitters
key_cards.notes: Works synergistically with Ethereal Armor and Ancestral Mask to create a giant unit.

key_cards.name: Ancestral Mask
key_cards.notes: Provides additional stats to the main creature.

key_cards.name: Armadillo Cloak
key_cards.notes: Practically a win condition against aggro decks, particularly red, by providing massive toughness.

key_cards.name: Utopia Sprawl
key_cards.notes: Ramps and fixes mana to play expensive auras and splash colors.

key_cards.name: Commune with Spirits
key_cards.notes: Fixes mana and provides card advantage, potentially finding the missing piece to win.

key_cards.name: Spirited Companion
key_cards.notes: Acts as an extra source of card advantage and interacts with other cards rather than serving as a main creature.

sequencing_tips: Curve well to maximize mana efficiency and take advantage of windows of opportunity
Play around counterspells and global removal by holding up mana or sequencing auras after threats are resolved
Prioritize surviving the early game against aggro before committing to the full combo
Mulligan aggressively if the hand cannot develop the game plan or dig for key pieces

Common mistakes include holding back a lethal attack, miscounting damage with Utopia Sprawl/Wild Growth, casting Malevolent Rumble too early without a target, and forgetting that Slippery Bogle can only be blocked by one creature.
Unique interactions include Utopia Sprawl's trample working with Wild Growth's power boost, and Malevolent Rumble being castable from the graveyard if you have enough mana.

matchups.advice: Considered favorable; Bogles deals more damage and gets a lot out of All That Glitters. Try to end the game as fast as possible.
Matchup defined by lifelink. With lifelink, survive 2-3 turns to win. Without it, rely on draws and hope opponent doesn't have key spells.
Run against time and play around Edicts. If opponent has an Edict and you have one creature, it's game over unless played around.
Similar to Gardens but with more control. Exploit Jeskai's slow early game to pressure the board and prepare the combo.
One of the worst matchups. Curve well and use Flaring Pain to break through fogs. Rely on luck and windows of opportunity.
Use Malevolent Rumble from the graveyard to bypass counters. Cast Utopia Sprawl/Wild Growth in response to their counterspells if you have priority. Use cheap threats to force interaction.
Outpace them by going wide/committing early. Use Lightning Strike/Abrade to remove their ramp sources. Don't let them stabilize; pressure relentlessly.
Use Lightning Strike/Abrade to remove key threats. Use Swords to Plowshares/Path to Exile to bounce or remove. Rely on your early damage to race them.
Apply pressure immediately. Use Malevolent Rumble to dig past their removal. Attack with everything you have. Force them to use multiple spells to stop one threat.
Win by having better card advantage (Malevolent Rumble) and better protection (Utopia Sprawl). Use Swords to Plowshares to remove their Bogles. Hold back a Sprawl/Growth to protect your threat.

common_threats: Watch for board wipes (Wrath of God, Damnation, etc.), targeted removal (Path, Lightning Strike), and graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Leyline).

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Bogles basically works like a combo deck... put many powerful auras on a creature with hexproof. It will eventually become a giant unit and deal lethal damage to your opponent.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: put many powerful auras on a creature with hexproof. It will eventually become a giant unit and deal lethal damage to your opponent.
Against aggressive opponents, you only need to survive until you can deal a lot of damage with one of your lifelink auras.
As for combo decks, you'll be able to play everything you want without any fear, but keep in mind time is an important factor.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Just curve well, and take advantage of your windows of opportunity.
Against Midrange and Control decks, you have to worry about counters and/or removals that can hit your creatures... and play around these cards as you develop your game plan.
Against aggressive opponents, you only need to survive until you can deal a lot of damage with one of your lifelink auras.
You must send back hands like the one above... risk isn't worth it.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Chainer’s Edict and similar cards... enchantment hates (for BW and BG), and counterspells (UB).
Dawnbringer Cleric , Destroy Evil , and Krark-Clan Shaman are the main cards Boros has against you
The worst-case scenario is if they have saved up Kuldotha Rebirth or Goblin Bushwhacker .
Ephemerate + Dawnbringer Cleric + Archaeomancer is simply devastating for Bogles, and Stonehorn Dignitary is even more impossible to beat.
Flaring Pain is perfect against Fogs. Considering the meta is full of Turbo Fogs
